class-files-and-instances.label: Class Files and Instances
class-files-and-instances.summary: Create classes in dedicated files and import instances into a runnable program.

try_class_files_and_instances_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_class_files_and_instances_sketch0.prompt: Create `models/student.py` with a `Student` class so `main.py` can import it and create one object.

try_class_files_and_instances_sketch1.title: Try it yourself
try_class_files_and_instances_sketch1.prompt: Fill in `models/profile.py` so each `Profile` stores its own `name` and `city`.

ci-contact-card-practice.title: Build a contact card model
ci-contact-card-practice.prompt: Complete the multi-file contact card. `Contact.label()` should return `Name <email>`.
ci-contact-card-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-contact-card-practice.starterCode:
from models.contact import Contact

contact = Contact("Ari", "ari@example.com")
print(contact.label())

sc-class-files-and-instances-design.title: Check the design choice
sc-class-files-and-instances-design.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for class files and instances?
sc-class-files-and-instances-design.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Create classes in dedicated files and import instances into a runnable program.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-class-files-and-instances-sketch0.title: Create a Student model file
try-class-files-and-instances-sketch0.prompt: Create `models/student.py` with a `Student` class so `main.py` can import it and create one object.
try-class-files-and-instances-sketch0.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-class-files-and-instances-sketch0.starterCode:
from models.student import Student

student = Student()
print("Student model ready")

try-class-files-and-instances-sketch1.title: Store profile instance state
try-class-files-and-instances-sketch1.prompt: Fill in `models/profile.py` so each `Profile` stores its own `name` and `city`.
try-class-files-and-instances-sketch1.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-class-files-and-instances-sketch1.starterCode:
from models.profile import Profile

profile = Profile("Maya", "Austin")
print(profile.name)
print(profile.city)

concept.title: Class Files and Instances: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Class Files and Instances** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Create classes in dedicated files and import instances into a runnable program.

multifile-shape.title: Class Files and Instances: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Class Files and Instances: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
